comment.body: In war times, Russian economy is way more adapt than EU one, and you can clearly see it. Of course both take the blow, but who is more resilient ?

comment.body: Yet DAX is up 10% YTD, while other European indices are also up on the year, as the S&P is down conversely 10%. Similarly EUR is up over 10% against the USD YTD, compounding the strength of its financial markets. A peculiar collapse, but all in the eye of the beholder, I suppose.

comment.body: It appears that any time you are asked to present evidence, you simply defer to another's rhetoric/viewpoint. Regardless, I'm very appreciative of your free speech: thankfully that is a feature of the society in which we live. As for 'Europe's collapse', well, GDP/employment/rates/currency would all counter that but ... your viewpoint is your viewpoint. Enjoy your day.
